q: ___ is the study of systems involving energy in the form of heat and work.
When two systems are in ___ equilibrium, there is no net heat transfer between them.
The ___ law of thermodynamics is a simple definition of thermodynamic equilibrium.
The first law of thermodynamics is an application of the ___ for heat and thermodynamic processes.
The change in internal energy $\Delta U$ of a system is equal to the net heat flow $Q$ into the system minus the total ___ $W$ done by the system.
$Q$ is positive if heat is ___ to the system.
$W$ is positive if work is done ___ the system.
The ___ $U$ of a system is defined as the sum of the energies of all the constituent particles.
The internal energy of ideal gases depends only on ___ , not on volume or pressure.
Internal energy of an ideal gas is directly proportional to the ___ .
___ and work are both energy in transit.
___ is a form of energy completely different from either heat or work.
A ___ may be defined as a change from one equilibrium state of a system to another.
In thermodynamics, a ___ process is a thermodynamic process that happens slowly enough for the system to remain in internal physical thermodynamic equilibrium.
An ___ process occurs at constant pressure.
In an isobaric process, the work is equal to the ___ of the shaded rectangle on a pressure Vs volume graph.
An ___ (isovolumetric) process occurs at constant volume.
In an isochoric process, no work is done by the system because $W = p \Delta V = 0$.
According to the first law, the change in internal energy must equal any ___ flowing into or out of the system in an isochoric process.
The symbol $c_v$ is ___ at constant volume.
The symbol $C_V$ is ___ at constant volume.
An ___ process occurs at a constant temperature.
For an ideal gas, internal energy $U$ is only a function of the ___ .
In an isothermal process, $\Delta U$ is equal to ___ .
In an ___ process, there is no heat transfer into or out of the system.
An adiabatic process can be done by enclosing the system in a ___ .
The first law of thermodynamics for an adiabatic process is reduced to $\Delta U = -W$.
The symbol $\gamma$ is the ratio of the ___ .
The temperature of a gas ___ when it is adiabatically compressed.
A process that does not satisfy reversible requirements is an ___ process.
